Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Sector Simple A basic-level cyber attack targeting the Healthcare and Public Health sector involves gaining initial access through phishing, executing malicious files, and causing impact by destroying critical data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Internal Email System, User Endpoints
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Social Engineering, File Execution

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An attacker sends a phishing email with a malicious attachment or link to lure unsuspecting employees into downloading and opening it.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 635; Name: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: Upon clicking the malicious link or opening the attachment, a malicious file executes, likely using reg.exe utility to run unauthorized commands.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675; Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The executed malware deletes or encrypts critical files, leading to data destruction and service disruption.

Scenario.summary: This scenario outlines a simple cyber attack chain involving phishing for initial access, execution of malicious files, and subsequent data destruction impacting the Healthcare and Public Health sector.
